summary: Completed comprehensive testing of NeuroBuddy AI Tutor Read Aloud (TTS) feature. Fixed critical bug in /api/chat/quick-action endpoint (422 error due to query params vs JSON body mismatch). All 14 backend API tests passed. Frontend TTS implementation using Web Speech API is working correctly - button states, per-message Read buttons, and error handling all verified.

fixed.issue: Returned 422 Unprocessable Entity - endpoint expected query parameters but frontend sent JSON body
fixed.fix_applied: Added QuickActionRequest Pydantic model to accept JSON body

expected_behaviors.observation: Shows 'Speech failed: synthesis-failed' toast in headless browser
expected_behaviors.note: Expected behavior - speechSynthesis API requires audio output device. Error handling is working correctly.

passed_tests: AI Tutor page loads correctly at /tutor after login
Read Aloud button is disabled when no AI messages exist (empty chat state)
Read Aloud button becomes enabled after an AI response is received
Per-message 'Read' button appears on each AI assistant message
Read Aloud button shows correct text ('Read Aloud' when idle)
Voice Input button renders correctly and is not disabled
Chat functionality works - sending message and receiving AI response
Quick action buttons (Explain Simpler, Give Example, Show Visually, Practice Problem, I'm Confused) all render after AI response
Quick action buttons work correctly - clicking 'Explain Simpler' generates new AI response
Follow-up suggestion chips render after AI response
TTS error handling works - shows appropriate toast message when speech fails
All 14 backend API tests passed (auth, chat, games, analytics, profile)
critical_code_review_comments: textToSpeech.js singleton pattern is well implemented with proper voice loading, error handling, and Chrome long-text bug workaround
AITutor.js correctly disables Read Aloud button when no AI messages exist using: disabled={messages.length === 0 || !messages.some(m => m.role === 'assistant')}
Per-message Read buttons correctly pass message content to handleReadAloud(msg.content)
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py - Fixed quick-action endpoint to accept JSON body instead of query params
seed_data_creation: None - used existing test student account
context_for_next_testing_agent: TTS feature is fully implemented using Web Speech API (frontend-only, no backend endpoint needed). The /api/voice/synthesize endpoint exists but is not used for TTS - it was the old implementation. speechSynthesis won't produce audio in headless browsers, so test UI state changes instead of actual audio output. Quick action endpoint was fixed to accept JSON body.
